name: Creamy Onion & Garlic Soup
recipeIngredient: 3 cups beef stock, or 3 cups broth, canned
3 tablespoons unsalted butter, at room temp
1 small onion, halved and thinly sliced
8 slices French bread (baguette), toasted to 1/2-inch thickness
recipeInstructions:
For soup:
1. Melt butter in a heavy large saucepan over medium heat.
2. Add onions and garlic. Saute until tender (do not brown), stirring occasionally for about 8 minutes.
3. Sprinkle flour over onions. Turn the heat to low.
4. Cook, stirring constantly, for 4 minutes.
5. Add wine. Bring to a boil.
6. Boil for 2 minutes.
7. Add stock and cream. Cook over medium heat until slightly thickened, stirring occasionally for about 20 minutes.
8. Season with salt and pepper. (Can be prepared up to 1 day ahead. Cover and refrigerate.)
For croutons:
1. Use a fork to blend 2/3 of the butter with cheese in a small bowl until smooth. Set aside.
2. Melt remaining butter in a small heavy skillet over medium heat.
3. Add onion. Saute until golden brown, stirring occasionally for about 12 minutes.
4. Add wine. Cook until liquid evaporates, stirring occasionally.
5. Transfer onion to another bowl. (Can be made up to 1 day ahead. Cover and chill cheese mixture and onion separately.)
6. Reheat soup over low heat if necessary.
7. Preheat the broiler.
8. Spread toasts with cheese mixture and top with onions. Broil until cheese melts and onion heats through.
9. Ladle soup into bowls. Garnish with toasts.
